changedFiles.reason: Defines the approved documentation scope, support-boundary intent, acceptance criteria, sources, and verification requirements.
Records the documentation changes, user direction, review evidence, and verification results.
Removes the detailed GitLab Free synthetic compatibility protocol and states that GitLab Free supports head-only diagnostics rather than this design's parallel merge safety guarantee.
Generated Cockpit status for the active documentation Work Item.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not imply that GitLab Free provides or will receive an equivalent parallel merge safety guarantee in this design.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The capability matrix and GitLab Free section state that only head-only diagnostics are available and parallel merge safety is not established.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep the unsupported-platform statement concise and capability-based rather than documenting a replacement implementation.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The former coordinator, credential, serialized push, retry, reconciliation, and integration-test protocol was replaced by two concise paragraphs.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Preserve the distinction between target-integrated candidate verification and head-only diagnostic checks.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Core candidate modes, the capability matrix, user-facing state, and end-to-end acceptance distinguish native target-integrated evidence from head-only diagnostics.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep AI Cockpit Core platform-neutral and avoid claiming runtime behavior is already implemented.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The Core contract remains candidate/base based, and the Status section continues to state that runtime, installer, and CI integration are not implemented.

risk.detail: The change narrows documentation claims without changing runtime behavior; review must confirm no contradictory GitLab Free synthetic support claims remain in the target design.

observedIssues: The prior design's GitLab Free synthetic coordinator could not bind mutable merge-request eligibility atomically to the final Git ref update, so equivalent parallel merge safety was overstated.
The prior design retained credential, protected coordinator, retry, and bookkeeping details even though the user no longer wants GitLab Free treated as a corresponding platform path.

reviewReadiness.reason: All required verification passed and no residual risk remains.
expectedReviewFocus: Confirm GitLab Free is explicitly head-only and never described as parallel-merge-safe.
Confirm GitHub Merge Queue and GitLab Premium or Ultimate Merge Train remain unchanged as the native supported paths.
Confirm no synthetic coordinator or credential implementation guidance remains.

userCorrectionsCaptured: GitLab Free does not need detailed coverage; state clearly that it does not correspond to this parallel merge safety design.
userCorrectionSolidification: The Goal and Decisions narrow supported parallel merge safety to GitHub Merge Queue and GitLab Premium or Ultimate Merge Trains.
The GitLab Free section, capability matrix, user-facing output, tests, and rollout sequence consistently classify GitLab Free as head-only diagnostics.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Reviewed the approved support boundary, exact documentation scope, native platform preservation, and explicit GitLab Free non-correspondence before editing.
Reviewed the completed support-boundary edit, removal of synthetic GitLab Free guidance, preservation of native platform paths, and absence of out-of-scope runtime changes before final verification.

knownGaps: No runtime adapter, installer profile, or CI integration is implemented by this documentation Work Item.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The design contains no GitLab Free synthetic coordinator or equivalent parallel merge safety claim.
evidence: Design Decisions, GitLab Free, Failure Semantics, Testing Strategy, and Rollout Sequence sections

scenarioCoverage.scenario: GitLab Free remains explicitly visible as head-only diagnostics rather than being silently omitted.
evidence: Design capability matrix, GitLab Free section, user-facing output, and end-to-end acceptance

scenarioCoverage.scenario: GitHub Merge Queue and GitLab Premium or Ultimate Merge Train remain the supported native platform paths.
evidence: Design Goal, Native Platform Adapters, Installation and Capability Detection, and Rollout Sequence sections

intentAlignment.rationaleValidated: The design now uses a concise capability boundary instead of a speculative GitLab Free compatibility protocol while preserving platform-neutral Core verification and native platform paths.

overclaimPrevention: Do not report completion for checks or behavior that were not verified.
